task:
Drag-to-ask DAEMON HALF (Oz hardening, item 3). The UI already prepends a pointer of the exact form `[context: <type> <id> — <label>]` (type ∈ priority|ticket|run) to the founder's message before sending it to the Oz daemon. The daemon does NOT yet parse or resolve that pointer. Implement the daemon half so Oz answers scoped to the dragged item BY REFERENCE (per founder design call #3: inject the item's FILE PATH with its slug shown — a cheap pointer, NOT the full file body).

OWNER MAP (already done — build at these owners, do NOT create a parallel contract):
- Pointer is produced by `contextLine()` in packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/dashboard/OzChat.tsx (line ~36): literal `[context: ${itemType} ${id} — ${label}]`. The em-dash is U+2014 (—). It is the FIRST line of the message; user text (if any) follows on the next line.
- Daemon prompt is assembled in `buildPrompt()` in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts (~line 190-209). The awareness snapshot (priorities, recentRuns/activeRuns, openTickets) is already loaded there via projectOzAwareness() (oz-awareness.ts) using readPriorities()/readTickets() (priority-order.ts) and ctx.store.listRuns(). REUSE these — do not add parallel loaders.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Parse the `[context: <type> <id> — <label>]` pointer out of the founder message body inside the daemon prompt path (buildPrompt or a small helper it calls). Match the EXACT shape the UI emits, including the U+2014 em-dash. Tolerate a message that has NO pointer (the common case) — leave behavior unchanged then.
2. Resolve the pointer's `id` to the item's ON-DISK FILE PATH plus its slug/label, reusing the already-loaded awareness data and the existing dir paths the daemon already knows (priorities dir, tickets dir, runs). For a priority → its Playbook file path; for a ticket → its file under cocoder/tickets/open/; for a run → its run-state location/identifier. If the id cannot be resolved, degrade gracefully (note it is unresolved) — never throw and never break the turn.
3. Inject a concise 'Requested context' section into the prompt that names the resolved type, id, slug/label, and FILE PATH, instructing Oz to read that file by reference to answer. Do NOT embed the file body. Keep the original user question in the prompt.

MUST NOT BREAK:
- Verb commands (launch/adhoc/show/status/stop/teardown) still route as before — this only affects the agent-turn prompt path.
- Messages with no `[context: ...]` pointer produce the identical prompt as today.
- The facts/awareness digest and transcript assembly are unchanged in shape.
- No new file I/O on the hot path beyond what's already loaded; resolve from in-memory awareness + known dir paths.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, judgment-gated): Add a daemon-side unit test (e.g. in packages/daemon/tests/) that feeds a founder message of the form `[context: priority <id> — <label>]\n<question>` and asserts the built prompt (a) contains a 'Requested context' section naming the resolved file path + slug, and (b) does NOT contain the file body; plus a case with no pointer asserting the prompt is unchanged from baseline. All existing daemon tests still pass. Provide the exact test command and its output as evidence.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/daemon/tests/**. Do not touch UI code (the UI half already landed). Match surrounding style; correctness first, then clarity, then elegance.

Build a one-command PROOF HARNESS for Oz-hardening items 2 & 4 (self-compaction on run-count + awareness reconstruction; auto-pickup of post-completion status changes). Items 2 & 4 landed in code (run_156) but have NO independent runnable proof yet — this converts the remaining proof to a runnable artifact (persona F18), it is NOT a live-app demo.

OWNERS to exercise (drive the REAL engine — do NOT mock or reimplement a parallel projection):
- `projectOzAwareness()` in packages/daemon/src/oz-awareness.ts — the single projection of priorities/runs/tickets that backs both refresh (item 2) and change-detection (item 4).
- The auto-compact path in packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts: `compactAfterOrchestratedRun(session, compactEveryRuns)` (~line 427) invoked via the handler at ~line 436, gated by setting `ozAutoCompactRuns` (packages/daemon/src/settings.ts, default 3, range 2–10). Drive compaction through the public entry point the daemon already uses after an orchestrated run settles — do not poke private state.
- The daemon store + ticket/priority/run loaders (readTickets/readPriorities/ctx.store.listRuns) already used by the projection.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. New file `scripts/proof-oz-awareness.mjs` matching the existing harness convention (see scripts/proof-oz-surfaces.mjs — mkdtemp temp workspace, real daemon APIs, prints PASS/FAIL per assertion, exits 0 on success / non-zero on any failure). Add a `proof:oz-awareness` entry to the root package.json scripts block (alongside the other proof:* entries).
2. ITEM 2 assertions (compact + reconstruct, no accuracy loss): set `ozAutoCompactRuns` low (e.g. 2) in the temp workspace settings; seed priorities/runs so there is at least one ACTIVE run; drive exactly that many orchestrated-run-settle events through the real handler so `compactAfterOrchestratedRun` fires; assert (a) compaction actually occurred (session transcript was compacted/cleared), and (b) a post-compaction status — `projectOzAwareness()` over the durable store — STILL returns the correct active-run summary reconstructed from durable state, NOT from chat history.
3. ITEM 4 assertions (auto-pickup without manual nudge): (a) write/commit a NEW ticket file under cocoder/tickets/open/ (the run_131 `0014` symptom) and assert it appears on the very next `projectOzAwareness()` refresh; (b) bring a run to wrap/teardown (settled/completed) and assert its outcome is reflected on the next refresh — both WITHOUT any manual re-fetch call beyond the normal refresh path.
4. Each assertion prints a clear `PASS: …` line naming what it proved; any failure prints `FAIL: …` with expected-vs-actual and the script exits non-zero.

MUST NOT BREAK / SCOPE:
- Do NOT change daemon runtime behavior. WRITE SCOPE: scripts/**, root package.json (proof:* entry only). If a function you must call is not exported, you MAY add a NARROW `export` keyword (no behavior change) in packages/daemon/src/** and must flag it in your summary — prefer the existing public entry points first.
- Keep it hermetic: temp workspace via mkdtemp, cleaned up; no dependency on a running daemon, Oz CLI, model, or the real cocoder/ tree.

EXIT CRITERION (one-shot, judgment-gated): `pnpm proof:oz-awareness` (equivalently `node scripts/proof-oz-awareness.mjs`) runs green and exits 0, printing PASS lines for: baseline projection; auto-compact-at-N fired; accurate post-compact active-run status from durable state (item 2); newly-added ticket reflected on next refresh; wrapped/settled run reflected on next refresh (item 4). Provide the exact command and its FULL stdout as evidence. Correctness first, then clarity, then elegance.
